divergences: Wallpaper is a macOS flow-ribbon photo; recreated with base fill + four edge-strip linear-gradients sampled every ~40 logical px from the reference photo along the visible left/right/top/bottom margins (TextEdit occludes the interior), each blurred and masked to fade toward the window, plus one extra radial-gradient patch for the bottom-right magenta swirl. The sharp diagonal ridge/fold lines and complex 2D swirl shapes in the real photo are approximated with soft blurred bands, not exact ridges — this residual is dominated by photographic detail and is the largest remaining error contributor.

Text rendering (antialiasing, subpixel hinting) differs slightly between the reference's real macOS/TextEdit rendering and the Chromium render used for the scene; visible only as thin edge outlines in the diff, no wrap or position mismatch remains.
TextEdit ruler ticks/marks approximated with repeating-linear-gradient layers rather than exact per-tick SVG; matches reference spacing and phase closely.
